title: Compile and link model deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Compile and link model: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? If separate .cpp files compile, declarations and definitions across them cannot violate a C++ program rule.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Translation units compile independently, but linked entities must still satisfy compatible declarations and the one-definition rule across the entire program.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Translation units compile independently, but linked entities must still satisfy compatible declarations and the one-definition rule across the entire program.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Translation units compile independently, but linked entities must still satisfy compatible declarations and the one-definition rule across the entire program.
If separate .cpp files compile, declarations and definitions across them cannot violate a C++ program rule.

answer.explanation: Translation units compile independently, but linked entities must still satisfy compatible declarations and the one-definition rule across the entire program.

items.title: Compile and link model: complete the language rule

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'one-definition rule'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Translation units compile independently, but linked entities must still satisfy compatible declarations and the one-definition rule across the entire program.
